content.text:
Here's what I found. **6 sites have WooCommerce/e-commerce content**, with varying depth:

---

## Sites With WooCommerce Content

### Faker Incorporated — Most Content-Rich
This is your best candidate. It has actual WooCommerce posts and products, not just the default pages:
- **"How to Get More Out of Your WooCommerce Store"** (post) — article about extending WooCommerce for clients
- **"Custom WooCommerce Store Build"** (product) — a WooCommerce product listing (Astra theme, speed/mobile optimized)
- Standard WC pages: Shop, Cart, Checkout, My Account

> Note: there appear to be 3 sites named "Faker Incorporated" (possibly different environments or duplicates) plus one named `faker-incorporated`. All have WooCommerce installed with the standard page set.

### The Curated Shelf
WooCommerce is installed with all the standard pages (Shop, Cart, Checkout, My Account). No custom posts found beyond the default WC pages — likely a newer/empty store setup.

### nexus-test-site / nexustestsite1
Both have WooCommerce standard pages (Shop, Cart, Checkout, My Account) but no custom post content — test sites with WooCommerce scaffolding.

### pm-toolkit-hub
Not a WooCommerce site — matched on e-commerce *topic* in PM planning docs (customer segment descriptions, TAM analysis referencing e-commerce site owners).

---

## Summary

| Site | WooCommerce Posts/Products | Store Pages |
|---|---|---|
| Faker Incorporated | Yes — 1 blog post + 1 product | Shop, Cart, Checkout, My Account |
| The Curated Shelf | No | Shop, Cart, Checkout, My Account |
| nexus-test-site | No | Shop, Cart, Checkout, My Account |
| nexustestsite1 | No | Shop, Cart, Checkout, My Account |

**Note:** All indexes are stale (>7 days). To get fresh results, run `reindex_site` on any of these before doing deeper content work.
